Judson McClendon wrote in message <5qU%2.84$9P4.71751@news3.mia>...
>Warren Porter wrote:
>>
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>
Warren Porter -- Remove numbers to reply.
```

#### ↳ Re: seek src to convert ebcdic to ascii files

- **From:** "Jeff" <a@a.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<GFG03.38$6y2.1416@storm.twcol.com>`
- **References:** `<19990515180018.14812.00000912@ng67.aol.com>`

```
>- convert downloaded binary Ebdcdic files in ascii files(and import in
Access
>or excel)


You may have problems with this one, if I read your posting correctly.
EBCIDIC to ASCII, to my understanding, is only valid for text files. You
state above that you are looking at "binary Ebdcdic". My interpretation of
that would be you have mixed text and binary data in your file. When you
convert the data with any simple utility it will convert the binary data
also, thus corrupting it.

You may be downloading your data incorrectly. To the best of my knowledge,
using FTP you have to set the download mode to BINARY to download the data
without EBCDIC to ASCII conversion. If your FTP session is in ASCII mode the
text as well as the binary data will be converted. Make sure you are in
BINARY mode then download the file again. I have worked on several different
IBM MVS mainframes and never had a problem, but they may use ASCII.
```

---

<a id="t-69bdb1f9e9"></a>
## Thread: COBOL Reengineering Tools — 6 msgs

### COBOL Reengineering Tools

- **From:** "Robert Williard" <rwilliard@home.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Wh4%2.57$Fz2.12@news.rdc1.az.home.com>`

```
Can anyone recommend COBOL reengineering tools that they have used (or stay
away from)?  I'm looking for tools that can help simplify the maintenance of
an old COBOL MVS application.

--Bob (RWilliard@Home.com)
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL Reengineering Tools

- **From:** William Lynch <WBLynch@att.net>
- **Date:** 1999-05-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<373D2F67.57239FCE@att.net>`
- **References:** `<Wh4%2.57$Fz2.12@news.rdc1.az.home.com>`

```
Robert Williard wrote:
> 
> Can anyone recommend COBOL reengineering tools that they have used (or stay
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> --Bob (RWilliard@Home.com)

I did a lot of this about 10 years ago and liked Retool a lot. If memory
(not RAM) serves, the company was CDSI in Rockville, MD. I had a lot of
experience with the Peat, Marwick product (a companion to PATHVU) whose
name escapes me now - I was underwhelmed. 

I'm sure all these products have evolved greatly over the years
(although I still prefer the 1990 version of Retool to IBM's CSF , circa
1995, a lot).

Email me privately if you're interested, I'm pretty sure I still have
the CDSI president's business card, which might make a good starting
point.

Bill Lynch

PS: Regarding reengineering services companies, avoid anything to do
with the Mellingers and/or Allen Stern like the plague infected with
Ebola (last name I knew was Platform Reengineering ...).
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: COBOL Reengineering Tools

- **From:** Arnold Trembley <arnold.trembley@worldnet.att.net>
- **Date:** 1999-05-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<373E316C.A2C50721@worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<Wh4%2.57$Fz2.12@news.rdc1.az.home.com> <373D2F67.57239FCE@att.net>`

```
William Lynch wrote:
> 
> Robert Williard wrote:
…[15 more quoted lines elided]…
> (snip)

I had some experience with IBM's CSF (COBOL Structuring Facility) in the
early 1990's.  It was set up to run from panels in TSO, and they were a
lot of options you could specify to your own tastes.  You could reformat
a program with all paragraphs as sections for example, or set you
indentation to 3 columns instead of 4.  I think there was some option to
let you split the program in a main with subprograms, but I never tried
that out.

I would have preferred a pure batch job setup, due to my lack of
experience with TSO.  I actually did use IBM CSF to restructure a few
programs that were installed back to production.  CSF could convert from
OS/VS COBOL to COBOL II (batch or CICS).  It would eliminate all the
GOTO's and ALTER's from your program.  It could add comments to a
paragraph indicating where it was called from.  IBM strongly recommended
you test the generated program to verify it still did exactly what the
old program did, but I never found any problems with that.

CSF would remove dead code.  You generally ended up with fewer paragraph
names that in the spaghetti-code input.  My first thought was this was
great tool, but there are disadvantages.  CSF would produce a correctly
structured program, but if the datanames and paragraph names were
cryptic in the input they were still cryptic in the output.  I was
hoping it would create a beautiful program from a trashy one, but all it
did was get rid of the spaghetti control flow.  There's still some value
in that.

They finally dropped the license for IBM CSF, because they couldn't find
anyone who ever used it except me.

I think there was a product called RECODER in the same time frame that
did essentially the same thing.  I don't remember the name of the
vendor, because I never got to evaluate the product, just saw the
colorful brochure.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: COBOL Reengineering Tools

- **From:** William Lynch <WBLynch@att.net>
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3740FC86.D613864E@att.net>`
- **References:** `<Wh4%2.57$Fz2.12@news.rdc1.az.home.com> <373D2F67.57239FCE@att.net> <373E316C.A2C50721@worldnet.att.net>`

```
Arnold Trembley wrote:
> 
> William Lynch wrote:
> >
(snip)

> I would have preferred a pure batch job setup, due to my lack of
> experience with TSO.  I actually did use IBM CSF to restructure a few
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> old program did, but I never found any problems with that.

I had problems with CSF in which the restructured source gave me compile
errors. I experimented with & gave up on CSF more than once (the product
was upgraded & improved - I'm not that big a masochist).

> CSF would remove dead code.  You generally ended up with fewer paragraph
> names that in the spaghetti-code input.  My first thought was this was
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
> colorful brochure.

IIRC, RECODER was one of the big four in COBOL restructuring tools,
which had a lot of play in the 2nd half of the 80's. I think RECODER
came from a company near Boston, whose name escapes me. It was heavily
oriented toward the application of algorithms to structure a program,
but the result wasn't very readable.

The Peat, Marwick product I couldn't remember was RETROFIT. The output
was pretty good, i.e., readable & structured; but the product didn't
handle some common problems, i.e., logic "knots".

SUPERSTRUCTURE was written by the guy (again, whose name escapes me) who
went on to produce RETOOL, after the original company was acquired by
CDSI. As I said before, I liked this product a lot.

The 4th entry was IBM's CSF.

Regards,
Bill Lynch
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL Reengineering Tools

- **From:** tim.oxler@NOSPAMteo-computer.com (Tim Oxler)
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3741753b.2226162@news.teo-computer.com>`
- **References:** `<Wh4%2.57$Fz2.12@news.rdc1.az.home.com>`

```
On Sat, 15 May 1999 01:42:14 GMT, "Robert Williard"
<rwilliard@home.com> wrote:

>Can anyone recommend COBOL reengineering tools that they have used (or stay
>away from)?  I'm looking for tools that can help simplify the maintenance of
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>


ViaSoft has this capability.  It's been a long time (@5yrs) since I've
used it.   It can help in tactical maintenance needs, but always be
careful.  

Re-engineering should always be carried out by a knowledgable
programmer/analyst.  Any re-engineering tool, is just that:  A tool.
How it is used, and how well it is used will vary from programmer to
programmer.



Tim Oxler
Teo Computer
http://www.teo-computer.com
Take the Y2k Quiz
http://www.teo-computer.com/dev/quiz.html
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: COBOL Reengineering Tools

- **From:** "Ira D. Baxter" <idbaxter@semdesigns.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3749A915.2C222C4A@semdesigns.com>`

> On Sat, 15 May 1999 01:42:14 GMT, "Robert Williard"
> <rwilliard@home.com> wrote:
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…

We offer re-engineering tools usable for COBOL, and as a demonstration, have used
them to implement
a code clone detector and remover.   See http://www.semdesigns.com.

Hi all,

I've been lurking and reading some, trying to gather some facts before I
open my mouth here.  I'm a PC person (web developer) but am just
starting to learn COBOL and don't have a mainframe to practice on.  I've
been searching the web for COBOL compilers and am pretty darned
confused. 

I saw a post telling the french student to go check out COBCy.  Is it a
good compiler? Are there others anyone can recommend?  Preferable free
as circumstances require at the moment.

After I download one and install it, do I use some other program to
write the source code in or can I use Notepad (like I can for HTML) and
then enter the file name in the compiler?   This is all done in MS-DOS I
am assuming, not under Windows, right?

Any help you can give is greatly appreciated.  Feel like I'm fumbling
around in the dark just a bit here.

:>  earthmom
```

- **From:** philhickling@my-dejanews.com

```
In article <7gchp4$bcb@news2.tds.net>,
  dginc@tds.net wrote:
> can anyone tell how to print my header at top of my page without it
defaulting
> about 4 lines down??  i have told it to advance 0 lines but it still
leaves
> about 3 or 4 lines skipped from top?
>
You can get closer to the top by sending printer control characters to
the printer, to set paper size, top and bottom margins, etc.  If you
remind me by sending me an email to philhickling@compuserve.com I could
try and hunt out some of the relevant HP PCL commands for you.

Hope this helps.

Phil

- **From:** philhickling@my-dejanews.com
- **Date:** 1999-05-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hkgbi$e1p$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<3728a479.19716098@news.texas.net> <7gap79$ebm$1@news.igs.net> <zCeW2.224$Hz1.10564@typhoon.mbnet.mb.ca>`

```
In article <zCeW2.224$Hz1.10564@typhoon.mbnet.mb.ca>,
  "Charles Goodman" <cgoodman@mbnet.mb.ca> wrote:
> That is just what I am looking for, but more.
> Do you know where I can get a full set of documentation on HP escape
…[32 more quoted lines elided]…
> >            02 FILLER               PICTURE X(11) VALUE
"h8.5v0s0b0T".
> >       *lines per inch
> >            02 FILLER               PICTURE X VALUE X"1B".
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> > Peter Gilchrist wrote in message
<3728a479.19716098@news.texas.net>...
> > >What is the WS for printing a page eject contriol sequence in an
> > >output document.
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…

does
> > >not work.  I think its the "comp-x" ... but I'm not sure what
> > >definition to use.
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>
Charles,
As someone else said, look in the printer manuals.  Some HP printers
come with good manuals that list all the control sequences, others dont.

In the hope that it helps I include a copy library that has most of my
printing stuff.  Look for the W99-ATR- fields.

      *
      **** ATRIBUTES FOR HP LASERJET 4P
       01  W99-ATR-HMI-03              PIC X(6) VALUE X"1B266B303348".
       01  W99-ATR-HMI-04              PIC X(6) VALUE X"1B266B303448".
       01  W99-ATR-HMI-05              PIC X(6) VALUE X"1B266B303548".
       01  W99-ATR-HMI-10              PIC X(6) VALUE X"1B266B313048".
       01  W99-ATR-HMI-20              PIC X(6) VALUE X"1B266B323048".
       77  W99-ATR-ESC                 PIC X    VALUE X"1B".
       77  W99-ATR-BOLD                PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B28733342".
       77  W99-ART-BOLD-A              PIC X(5) VALUE "@B--@".
       77  W99-ATR-NORMAL              PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B28733042".
       77  W99-ATR-EXTRA-BOLD          PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B28733742".
       77  W99-ATR-PORTRAIT            PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266C304F".
       77  W99-ATR-LANDSCAPE           PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266C314F".
       77  W99-ATR-SIMPLEX             PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266C3053".
       77  W99-ATR-DUPLEX-LONG         PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266C3153".
       77  W99-ATR-DUPLEX-SHORT        PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266C3253".
       77  W99-ATR-ELITE-12            PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266B3453".
      **** WARNING:  ROMAN 8 ONLY SHOWS � IF � IS REPLACED BY POUND-SIGN
       77  W99-SS-ROMAN-8              PIC X(4) VALUE X"1B283855".
       77  W99-SS-PC-8                 PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B28313055".
      **** THE FOLLOWING 3 HAVE � IN POSITION 163
      *77  W99-SS-windows              PIC X(4) VALUE X"1B283955".
      *77  W99-SS-ps-text              PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B28????55".
      *77  W99-SS-ecma-94              PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B28????55".
       77  W99-ATR-FIXED               PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B28733050".
       77  W99-ATR-PROPORTIONAL        PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B28733150".
       77  W99-ATR-COMPRESSED          PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266B3253".
       77  W99-ATR-LINES-PER-3         PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266C3344".
       77  W99-ATR-LINES-PER-4         PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266C3444".
      *77  W99-ATR-LINES-PER-5         PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266C3544".
       77  W99-ATR-LINES-PER-6         PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266C3644".
       77  W99-ATR-LINES-PER-8         PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B266C3844".
       77  W99-ATR-LINES-PER-PAGE-66   PIC X(6) VALUE X"1B266C363646".
       77  W99-ATR-RESET               PIC XX   VALUE X"1B45".
       77  W99-ATR-ITALIC              PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B28733153".
       77  W99-ATR-UPRIGHT             PIC X(5) VALUE X"1B28733053".
      *970521 - TRY UL ON RATHER THAN FLOATING UL ON

       77  W99-ATR-UL-ON               PIC X(4) VALUE X"1B266444".
       77  W99-ATR-UL-OFF              PIC X(4) VALUE X"1B266440".
      **** TEST:  SET SHORT EDGE OFFSET
       01  W99-ATR-SHORT-EDGE-OFFSET-00 PIC X(6) VALUE X"1B266C30305A".
       01  W99-ATR-SHORT-EDGE-OFFSET-01 PIC X(6) VALUE X"1B266C30315A".
       01  W99-ATR-SHORT-EDGE-OFFSET-02 PIC X(6) VALUE X"1B266C30325A".

      **** TYPEFACES
       77  W99-TF-CGTIMES              PIC X(17) VALUE
           X"1B281B2838551B287331701B2873313056".

           X"1B281B2838551B287331701B28733431343854".

<a id="t-3fab7829b7"></a>
## Thread: VS-COBOL version 1.2 MANUAL (CIRCA 1980) — 2 msgs

### VS-COBOL version 1.2 MANUAL (CIRCA 1980)

- **From:** "Mias Strydom" <emias@iafrica.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hkpj4$1s6o$1@nnrp01.ops.uunet.co.za>`

```
Hi - Does anybody perhaps know if there is any online
Info/manuals/Y2K/Anything available on VS-COBOL 1.2 somewhere?
Thanks Mias
```

#### ↳ Re: VS-COBOL version 1.2 MANUAL (CIRCA 1980)

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hkt8j$e8v@dfw-ixnews3.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<7hkpj4$1s6o$1@nnrp01.ops.uunet.co.za>`

```
If you mean IBM's "OS/VS COBOL - Release 2.4 - or Release 2.3" then the
answer is NO, an online version doesn't exist.  These manuals weren't even
done with any facility that COULD be put online (unless you wanted to "scan"
each page).

If you are looking for online information on migrating from OS/VS COBOL (or
DOS/VS COBOL) to any of IBM's current products (VS COBOL II R4.0 or IBM
COBOL for OS/390 & VM), then the migration material is online.  Let us know
if this is what you are looking for, and I can point you to the right place.
```

---

<a id="t-a65b7930a0"></a>
## Thread: seek program to see a file with its COPY — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax)_

### seek program to see a file with its COPY

- **From:** zornfr999@aol.com (Zornfr999)
- **Date:** 1999-05-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990515180457.14812.00000914@ng67.aol.com>`

```
Hello, bonjour

I'm looking for a program that analyse any COPY and display a file with it, on
PC.
(the same as File-Aid on MVS).

Bye.
```

#### ↳ Re: seek program to see a file with its COPY

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-15T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hkt16$e20@dfw-ixnews3.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<19990515180457.14812.00000914@ng67.aol.com>`

```
Compuware sells a PC version of File-Aid.  If that is what you are used to
on the mainframe, why not use it on the PC?  (Of course Micro Focus and
several others ALSO sell products that do the same thing.  In fact with
Micro Focus, if you have "Workbench" or "Mainframe/Express" - ;you already
own this feature, via their "data file editor".)
```

---

<a id="t-631dd5c4a1"></a>
## Thread: Micro Focus Personal Cobol Compiler — 13 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Micro Focus Personal Cobol Compiler

- **From:** "Alan J Farrington" <alan@farrington.u-net.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<eFw%2.1053$T3.2940@newsr2.u-net.net>`

```
With apologies to all if this is a inappropriate posting to the group.

I would like to teach myself COBOL and have acquired the SAMS publication
"Teach yourself Cobol in 21 days" - somewhat optimistic I think !.

The problem of course is that I now need to obtain a compiler, preferably
the recommended one Micro Focus Personal Cobol. Having had a look around
their web site it rather unhelpfully does not give a price (which in my
experience is always a bad sign) but suggests I ring them.

Does anyone use such a compiler and have any indication as to it's cost or
alternatively could they recommend another. Obviously the cheaper the
better - preferably free :-)

Any other suggestions or opinions re a programming language to learn with a
view to future employment prospects would be very welcome.

Thanks in anticipation folks.

Alan
```

#### ↳ Re: Micro Focus Personal Cobol Compiler

- **From:** fwkirk@ibm.net (Frank Kirk)
- **Date:** 1999-05-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9ddCdfeFk555-pn2-rLdmtdQBGxJ0@localhost>`
- **References:** `<eFw%2.1053$T3.2940@newsr2.u-net.net>`

```
On Sun, 16 May 1999 09:56:55, "Alan J Farrington" 
<alan@farrington.u-net.com> wrote:

> I would like to teach myself COBOL and have acquired the SAMS publication
> "Teach yourself Cobol in 21 days" - somewhat optimistic I think !.
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> experience is always a bad sign) but suggests I ring them.

Alan,

	I bought the same book about a year ago and a compiler was included 
in the back of the book on a diskette.  Acucobol-85.
Have you tried contacting the publisher to see if it was inadvertently
omitted from your book?

FWK
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Micro Focus Personal Cobol Compiler

- **From:** coboljit@aol.com (COBOLJIT)
- **Date:** 1999-05-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990517142419.08343.00002675@ngol02.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<9ddCdfeFk555-pn2-rLdmtdQBGxJ0@localhost>`

```
In article <9ddCdfeFk555-pn2-rLdmtdQBGxJ0@localhost>, fwkirk@ibm.net (Frank
Kirk) writes:

>
>	I bought the same book about a year ago and a compiler was included 
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>

The COBOL Started kit included the 1st edition of Teach Yourself COBOL in 21
Days and the AcuCOBOL Compiler - both on a CD.  No Compiler came with the book
as at the time it was written no companies were making compilers available to
be included with a book.
Helen

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
King Computer Services, Inc.
COBOL Training for the Year 2000
"COBOL, Just in Time" Crash Course
"COBOL, Dates and the Year 2000" Technical Reference.
818-951-5240
```

- **From:** fwkirk@ibm.net (Frank Kirk)
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9ddCdfeFk555-pn2-tqW6PPhymQZD@localhost>`
- **References:** `<9ddCdfeFk555-pn2-rLdmtdQBGxJ0@localhost> <19990517142419.08343.00002675@ngol02.aol.com>`

```
On Mon, 17 May 1999 18:24:19, coboljit@aol.com (COBOLJIT) wrote:

> The COBOL Started kit included the 1st edition of Teach Yourself COBOL in 21
> Days and the AcuCOBOL Compiler - both on a CD.  No Compiler came with the book
> as at the time it was written no companies were making compilers available to
> be included with a book.

	You're indeed correct!  I apologize to the original poster for my 
erroneous answer.  

	I had also bought a book by Sams, "C Programming in 12 Easy Lessons" 
which had a diskette included in the back of the book.  I got the two 
mixed up.

FWK
```

#### ↳ Re: Micro Focus Personal Cobol Compiler

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-05-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<373eeb3b.230075848@news1.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<eFw%2.1053$T3.2940@newsr2.u-net.net>`

```
On Sun, 16 May 1999 10:56:55 +0100, "Alan J Farrington"
<alan@farrington.u-net.com> wrote:

>With apologies to all if this is a inappropriate posting to the group.
>
>I would like to teach myself COBOL and have acquired the SAMS publication
>"Teach yourself Cobol in 21 days" - somewhat optimistic I think !.
>

I know it sounds even more optimistic, but I suggest you pick up my
book, Sams Teach Yourself COBOL in 24 Hours.  It includes a compiler.

Check out the Table of Contents and a sample chapter at:

http://www.barnesandnoble.com

It really is 24 1 hour lessons.  I wrote it to teach "Current" COBOL
from the ground up.
```

#### ↳ Re: Micro Focus Personal Cobol Compiler

- **From:** "Moore" <moochie@saol.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<01bea041$687a0c60$60e619c4@moore>`
- **References:** `<eFw%2.1053$T3.2940@newsr2.u-net.net>`

```
Hi 

I am also currently doing a course using the book you are using.  However,
I am doing it as part of a home study course which  I bought on the
internet.  The course is offered by King Computer Associates in Tujunga,
California.  This company belongs to Mo Budlong, the author of Teach
Yourself Cobol in 21 Days.  Along with this book, the course gives you the
MF Personal Compiler, a course guideline, a COBOL cookbook, printer spacing
sheets, etc (basically all you need to get through the book).  I believe
you should be able to get the MF compiler directly from King Computer.  I
think it costs about $100.  I have found it to be an excellent learning
tool.

I am also, concurrently, reading "COBOL for Dummies" (which has some useful
stuff on Y2K) and using a disk based learning tool called "Mastering COBOL"
which is offered by John Wiley and Sons (also very useful).

I am (almost) a qualified patent attorney, but have packed it all in to
work on the Y2K problem (hence the need for COBOL).  I am running on gut
instinct here as I have no jobs lined up, or even ideas bout where to look
for them.....yet.  But I think the demand will be there and the money good.
 I look forward to keeping in touch with you, regarding how the course is
going, and job prospects.

BTW, I have found a couple of minor problems with the MF Personal compiler,
and maybe someone reading this can help me out.  After compiling, the
"checker" is supposed to list the syntax errors found.  However, mine
immediately defaults back to the editor screen, so I can't get an overall
list of the errors.  Any ideas about what is going on?

Thanks,

Rory Moore.
 

Alan J Farrington <alan@farrington.u-net.com> wrote in article
<eFw%2.1053$T3.2940@newsr2.u-net.net>...
> With apologies to all if this is a inappropriate posting to the group.
> 
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> Does anyone use such a compiler and have any indication as to it's cost
or
> alternatively could they recommend another. Obviously the cheaper the
> better - preferably free :-)
> 
> Any other suggestions or opinions re a programming language to learn with
a
> view to future employment prospects would be very welcome.
> 
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

- **From:** "Moore" <moochie@saol.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

- **References:** `<eFw%2.1053$T3.2940@newsr2.u-net.net> <01bea041$687a0c60$60e619c4@moore>`

```
Hi

Me again.  I have one other problem with Personal COBOL.  When I print a
source listing from the Editor, two lines of code get 
left off the top of the second, third, etc. pages.  I have a Canon BJC
bubble jet printer.  Any idea how I can fix this?

Cheers

Rory Moore.

compiler,

### Need help! for COBOL user compant

- **From:** leelawan@my-dejanews.com
- **Date:** 1999-05-16T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hl7bg$sob$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Dear Friends,

I am doing an assigment about COBOL. I have to find out the user of
COBOL programm in Australia. I have tried to search from the website,
but most of the result are the COBOL software sales company. It would be
obliged if there is anybody help me to find out. Please let me know if
you have this information or any suggestion to get this information. My
assignemnet dues the end of May. Thank you for your attention.

Regards,
Leelawan


--== Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/ ==--
---Share what you know. Learn what you don't.---
```

```
leelawan,

What do you want to know,  I am in Australia and can probably
answer most of your questions and give you some pointers.  You
possibly will not find out anthing from the Web.  My company
supports it's web application by Cobol code but never mentions
Cobol in it's pages.  There are to many biggots to offend.

Ken

```
Dear Ken,
Thank you for your help. The topic of my assignment is about "Do you
believe that COBOL often comes under critcism with for being outdated?".
The lecture exprcted to research the statistic or information:
     - A survey of local business (both large and small) to identify
what language they are using
     - Survey job adds in local and Natinal newspaper for business
programmers
     - Survey Universities in Australia and worldwidw toidentify what
language they are using for business programming
I have found some information of the 2nd and 3rd question. But the first
one, I do not know what I shoul do. Some of my friends told me to send
randomly the e-mail to the companies, which I think it will take time to
get the answer and I do know what companies I shoul send. I am an
international student. Do you have any suggestion ? I think I will do
only Australia. Please let me know.

Thanks,
Leelawan

- **From:** docdwarf@clark.net ()

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<uAU03.69$l7.295@iad-read.news.verio.net>`
- **References:** `<7hl7bg$sob$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <%hY%2.935$Ku4.32372@iad-read.news.verio.net> <7i0dih$evo$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
In article <7i0dih$evo$1@nnrp1.deja.com>,  <leelawan@my-dejanews.com> wrote:
>In article <%hY%2.935$Ku4.32372@iad-read.news.verio.net>,
>  docdwarf@clark.net () wrote:
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>I hope you will read this mail.

This reply might possibly be an indication that your hope was fulfilled.

>Thank you for you encouragement.

You're welcome.

>Of
>course, I will do my homework.

From your original request there was little 'of course' I could see.

>What I would like is more information
>that I have found some from the web and other sources.

There is a difference between looking for information and asking others to
supply you with information.

>I was suggested
>from my friend that newsgroup would be the other source.

Sometimes even the smartest friends make mistakes.

>Do you think
>newsgroup can do your homework?

I know that other people have asked it to; I have given them the same
reply as I gave you.

>You are so mean.

I included a 'please'... if you want chocolate and roses you';ll have to
find them somewhere else.

>I am so sorry that my
>mail annoyed you so much.

No 'so much' involved... you are not the first and I am sure that you will
not be the last.

>Please forgive the newuser who does not know
>what newsgroup is for.

It is my pleasure to have given you some learning about 'what newsgroup is
for'... although a 'thank you' from you might be appropriate.

Now, please do your own homework.

DD
```

Mr Foskey, it seems as though we disagree about what constitutes

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Need help! for COBOL user compant

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7ic68g$ain@dfw-ixnews12.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<7hl7bg$sob$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <7i0dih$evo$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <uAU03.69$l7.295@iad-read.news.verio.net> <374994F4.F13093B7@zip.com.au> <IHg23.317$l7.5024@iad-read.news.verio.net>`

```
<docdwarf@clark.net> wrote in message
news:IHg23.317$l7.5024@iad-read.news.verio.net...
> In article <374994F4.F13093B7@zip.com.au>,
> Ken Foskey  <waratah@zip.com.au> wrote:
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Although I have stayed out this particular thread, "do your own homework" is
one of the places that I frequently disagree with DD on.  I am happy to
provide whatever help I can - to someone who demonstrates that they want
HELP with their homework rather than for me to do it for them.  This is why
I try to reply (positively) to anyone who shows their work or how they got
to their current state of confussion.  On the other hand, I usually ignore
those who simply ask us to provide them with the solution to the original
assignment.  This is often a "fine line" - but I thought that the original
poster of this question indicated

   A) that they were doing homework
   B) why they were posting to the NG
   C) what they had done to get to this point

To me this "met" all the pre-requisites for an entirely appropriate
question/request for the NG.  I understand DD's "objection" but am pleased
that many others (with more current input that I could provide) did give the
original poster some good input and replies.

I'll never convince DD that  requests for HELP are valid - and he will never
convince me that all homework assistance requests indicate students not
doing their own work.

Isn't it wonderful that we can "disagree" on this -  but still both
particpate actively and positively in this NG!
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Need help! for COBOL user compant

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** docdwarf@clark.net ()
- **Date:** 1999-05-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<%yy23.384$l7.7380@iad-read.news.verio.net>`
- **References:** `<7hl7bg$sob$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <374994F4.F13093B7@zip.com.au> <IHg23.317$l7.5024@iad-read.news.verio.net> <3749ADF1.891514BD@zip.com.au>`

```
In article <3749ADF1.891514BD@zip.com.au>,
Ken Foskey  <waratah@zip.com.au> wrote:
>docdwarf@clark.net wrote:
>> 
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>We are formal this morning...

We are always formal... and we remember that plural majestatus est, too!

>
>I am actually in the process of getting the peice of paper and one
>of the research references that you were allowed is news groups.

Ahhhhh... Mr Foskey, I believe that there is a difference between using a
newsgroup as a reference source and using the people in a newsgroup as
researchers; consider the following statements:

'A DejaNews PowerSearch of comp.lang.cobol showed...'

'I need to find (x) for my homework assignment...'

>He requested someone for a reference site and highlighted that he
>did not just lob in without ground work.

'Ground work'... hmmmm, now there's a telling phrase.  Perhaps 'ground
work' might consist only of the most basic of Altavista searches,
returning billions of web-pages?  You see the difficulties, I assume...
consider 'Well, I actually *did* go into the library and *boy*, there's a
lot of books there... so I asked my cousin, the PhD, to do it for me.'

>
>I personally know that getting information on what people are
>actually using is difficult.  You really have to know someone who
>knows someone or else you just get dumb res

Precisely... and *that* might have been the lesson to be learned from the
homework, *not* the information retrieved.

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Need help! for COBOL user compant

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** docdwarf@clark.net ()
- **Date:** 1999-05-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<nyX23.524$l7.11015@iad-read.news.verio.net>`

donald tees <donald@willmack.com> wrote:
>
>docdwarf@clark.net wrote in message ...
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>Surely *asking* is a reasonable reasearch method?

Mr Tees, that is precisely the point... the 'asking' of whom, for what, is
another matter entire.  Granted that, as should be obvious, the time I've
spent in the Hallowed Groves of Academe is minimal but I cannot recall
ever receiving an assignment which began with 'Go and ask somebody to tell
you about...'; even the Social Sciences have the delicatesse to rephrase
this into 'conduct a poll or survey concerning'... then again, my memory
is, admittedly, porous.

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Need help! for COBOL user compant

- **From:** MKS <m_k_s@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<374419B4.4332632A@hotmail.com>`
- **References:** `<7hl7bg$sob$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <%hY%2.935$Ku4.32372@iad-read.news.verio.net> <7i0dih$evo$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Leelewan,

I think your question was quite appropriate for a newsgroup.  You might
try other news groups and see if you get more input from them.  Search
through a wide variety of different groups (e.g. Australian groups, or
Business groups, etc.).  Also, have you tried doing a search in the
various engines (Altavista, Yahoo, Excite, etc.) on your topic?

Please excuse Dr. Dwarf.  I think he is much better than the rest of us
and was bothered by your question.  If you do a sort on userid and check
his last posts you will find that each is full of technical wisdom and
profound knowledge that I am still trying to digest (I try to study one
each night).  It is the plight of the superior to have to deal with
annoying, trivial questions that we, the common folk, might have.  As
long as you understand your place and give DD his due respect we should
all get along fine.  

MKS
```

wrote:
> >Dear Friends,

…[6 more quoted lines elided]…

Doc -

I think in this case the poster WAS doing their homework.  He wasn't
asking for someone to write his code or explain that the error msg
means what it says.  He was tasked with gathering information, and he
was asking for sources.  Kinda like walking into a cellulose based
library and asking the person at the desk on which floor the microfilm
newspaper archives could be found.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Need help! for COBOL user compant

- **From:** docdwarf@clark.net ()
- **Date:** 1999-05-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<6RU03.70$l7.295@iad-read.news.verio.net>`
- **References:** `<7hl7bg$sob$1@nnrp1.deja.com> <%hY%2.935$Ku4.32372@iad-read.news.verio.net> <7i12o3$t2g$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
In article <7i12o3$t2g$1@nnrp1.deja.com>,
Ed Stevens  <Ed.Stevens@nmm.nissan-usa.com> wrote:
>In article <%hY%2.935$Ku4.32372@iad-read.news.verio.net>,
>  docdwarf@clark.net () wrote:
…[18 more quoted lines elided]…
>newspaper archives could be found.

Mr Stevens, from the description of the assignment I think that the kind
of basic research you describe - finding the library, locating the right
desk, learning how to request microfilmed or fiche'd archives - was
*exactly* the set of tasks which was being assigned.

Our opinions may differ, of course... but I recall, years ago, when my
Younger Brother spent a college summer living with my Eldest Brother; YB
was working as a coder and taking some more programming courses
more-or-less simultaneously.  One of these courses was Pure Hell, a
six-week torment which involved learning C, Pascal and Modula-2 (or some
similar trio of languages)

When it was over Eldest Brother asked 'So, what was the most important
thing you learned?' Younger Bro began to recite a litany of languages,
structures, processes, blah de blah, quack de woof... and Elder Bro cut
him off with:

'No, those are the incidentals... the most important thing you learned was
how to learn a programming language in two weeks.'

Likewise, Mr Stevens, folks rarely learn how to do their own research by
having others do this research for them.. and this is what I saw, rightly
or wrongly so, in the request.

DD
```

associated with the position(s) offered; doing otherwise leads many to

determine rates," I'm beginning to think you may not be a real person.

Are you a mailbot?

Michael Mattias
Tal Systems
Racine WI USA
michael.mattias@gte.net

des champs soit comp-1, soit comp-3.
    Pourriez-vous �ventuellement me faire conna�tre le code d' un programme
( cobol, je suppose ) qui me permettrait de d�chiffrer les fichiers cr��s
par cobol ( pour ensuite me permettre de l' �diter sous une autre forme et
de le traiter avec un autre logiciel - Access ou ACL par exemple)?

#### ↳ Re: Where can I get a COBOL compiler?

- **From:** reinhold@world.std.com
- **Date:** 1999-05-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7huilg$3pb$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`
- **References:** `<373e4385.55240395@news.mindspring.com>`

```
In article <373e4385.55240395@news.mindspring.com>,
  twozoogs@mailexcite.com (zoogs) wrote:
> I tried the Fujitsu site and it seems that now that v4 is out they
> don't offer a free compiler. Any help appreciated.
>

Mastering COBOL from Sybex (list price $49.99) comes with a copy of the
Fujitsu COBOL version 4 compiler, PowerCOBOL for creating Windows GUIs
and Casegen COBOL for Windows, a tool for generating full-function
Windiws applications. You can get Mastering COBOL at a discount from
Amazon.com:
http://www.amazon.com/exec/obidos/ASIN/078212321X/templebethshalom

The book covers a lot of material besides the COBOL language itself and
is aimed and intermediate to advanced users.

Arnold Reinhold (who contributed a couple of chapters)


--== Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/ ==--

```

<a id="t-c4eccdd365"></a>
## Thread: RM/Cobol-85 and Windows NT — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### RM/Cobol-85 and Windows NT

- **From:** linalm@ibm.net
- **Date:** 1999-05-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hpges$lq7$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Hi there!

We have been working with RM/Cobol-85 for a long time and now we are
facing a problem that we don't understand. We wonder if any one of you
have had a similar problem or can give us ideas about what it can be
causing the problem and how we can solve it. Thanks in advance for your
time and comments. Here is the description of the situation:

The program with the problem is run by an application that handles
about 80 different files, most of them being indexed. By the time the
program is executed, approximately 15 files are already opened. The
program opens 30 files, 15 of which are opened in I/O mode. One of
those files, the main one that is used in the process, has 22 keys, 640
bytes per record, and approximately 5 megabytes in total disk space.
The program can be executed either under Unix or under Windows. When
the program is executed under UNIX, everything works fine, and when we
run it under Windows, the performance is terrible, processing time
increase over 10 times the time we get under UNIX! ! . The user has
Windows 95 on his workstation and Windows NT as the software for the
network. The Windows NT is optimized. To alleviate the problem a little
bit, we have moved the files to another disk and that has helped for
just a few days, then, everything goes back to a bad performance. Since
the same program with the same data, works very well under UNIX and the
Windows NT is optimized, we don�t really know what it can be causing
the problem and how we can solve it. The machine where the software
runs under Windows is a Compaq proliant 1600 Pentium II with 450 MHz,
192 Megabytes RAM, raid 5 disks and a total of 27 gigabytes in disk
space.

We think that the problem can be with the NT, due to some restrictions
that may apply and we don�t know. Does anyone of you have any idea or
knows something about problems executing a RM/Cobol-85 program under
Windows NT 4.0? The RM/Cobol version we use is 6.5.

Thanks very much.

Lina Maria Londono




--== Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/ ==--
---Share what you know. Learn what you don't.---
```

#### ↳ Re: RM/Cobol-85 and Windows NT

- **From:** Gerald Logan <loganj@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1999-05-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<374D924F.20A0F97A@bellsouth.net>`
- **References:** `<7hpges$lq7$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
The problem is most likely due to doing file i-o across the network.  Where
do the files actually reside?  I suspect that the files are on the unix
server where the cobol does not have to go across the network to retrieve
the data.  When running under windows each i-o to the file has to transfer
the data across the network before the program can do anything with it.

linalm@ibm.net wrote:

> Hi there!
>
…[37 more quoted lines elided]…
> ---Share what you know. Learn what you don't.---
```

---

On Mon, 17 May 1999 12:44:44, "tommy" <tommynilsen@yahoo.com> wrote:

I'm not familiar enough with the reports created by Crystal reports to
answer.  Send an e-mail to info@flexus.com and ask them about this 
specific issue.

However, to answer the question as best I can.  FormPrint excels at 
printing "pages" that you layout usiing the form editor.  HOWEVER, I 
have used it to print to the generic text printer as if it were a line
printer by defining a single field on a form.  You can print a "page" 
of data easily by either using a multi-line text field or by defining 
your own "lines" on the form.


> I've taken a quick look at FormPrint from Flexus , and I have one question.
> Can FormPrint be used to print anything other than forms ??
…[24 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

-------------------------
Trust the computer industry to shorten "Year 2000" to Y2K.  It was 
this
kind of thinking that caused the problem in the first place.

Visit my updated website at
http://www.geocities.com/Eureka/2006/
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Microfocus and PC_PRINTER_.... calls

- **From:** rtwolfe@flexus.com (Bob Wolfe)
- **Date:** 1999-05-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<37407b89.25963443@news.enter.net>`
- **References:** `<7hnls2$c83$1@nntp.newmedia.no> <pdU%2.197$R96.568@news1.online.no>`

```
"tommy" <tommynilsen@yahoo.com> wrote:

>I've taken a quick look at FormPrint from Flexus , and I have one question.
>Can FormPrint be used to print anything other than forms ??
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>Tommy

Tommy:

Sorry, FormPrint wasn't designed to work with Crystal Reports.

Some of our customers use FormPrint to print reports, but the product
was originally designed to be a "page oriented" printing product.

If things change, I'll post it here.


Bob Wolfe, flexus
Check out The Flexus COBOL Page at http://www.flexus.com
```

```
In article <7hnls2$c83$1@nntp.newmedia.no>,
  "tommy" <tommynilsen@yahoo.com> wrote:
> Does anyone know of a software-component (DLL) that deals with
printing ??
> (Like PowerMail and PowerReg...)
>
> The reason I'm asking about this is that I can't get the
PC_PRINTER_...
> calls in Netexpress 3.0 from Microfocus to work properly.
> The API's works fine with printers attached to a Novell-server, but
is not
> working at all with printers attached to a NT-server (won't return
> printer-name).
>
> Tommy

Tommy,

I don't know anything about FormPrint nor Crystal reports, but the
NetExpress PC_PRINTER_ routines should work.  I used to use
PC_PRINT_FILE to print the whole file which I formated elsewhere, but
have been advised by tech support that PC_PRINTER_OPEN,
PC_PRINTER_WRITE etc are better.  They work.  The only problem I have
had is with special control characters not getting converted under NT.
It may be a problem with my printer drivers, but since it worked under
W95 with PC_PRINT_FILE I think it should work under NT with
PC_PRINTER_WRITE.
> tommynilsen@yahoo.com
>
>


--== Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/ ==--
---Share what you know. Learn what you don't.---
```

## Thread: COBOL Standards — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`COBOL standards (ANS, ISO, 74/85/2002/2014)`](topics.md#standards)_

### COBOL Standards

- **From:** coboljit@aol.com (COBOLJIT)
- **Date:** 1999-05-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990517142423.08343.00002678@ngol02.aol.com>`

```
Does anyone know where I might find a summary of the changes in COBOL
introduced by COBOL 85, and a summary of the changes being looked at currently
for the next revision?
Helen

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
King Computer Services, Inc.
COBOL Training for the Year 2000
"COBOL, Just in Time" Crash Course
"COBOL, Dates and the Year 2000" Technical Reference.
818-951-5240
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL Standards

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-05-17T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Jl0PnHJ5PvPd-pn2-e2rGfYmW8LDt@Dwight_Miller.iix.com>`
- **References:** `<19990517142423.08343.00002678@ngol02.aol.com>`

```
On Mon, 17 May 1999 18:24:23, coboljit@aol.com (COBOLJIT) wrote:

> Does anyone know where I might find a summary of the changes in COBOL
> introduced by COBOL 85, and a summary of the changes being looked at currently
> for the next revision?
> Helen

Scan the group in Deja for the last week or so for a post by Bill 
Klein about "Everything you ever wanted to know about...".  It has a 
link to the current draft standard, which at the end has an appendix 
with the differences.

The best thing I have found for Cobol85 vs '74 is Don Nelsons book, 
Cobol 85 for Programmers.  I don't have Don's present e-mail, but 
again, scan Deja (not news, now, just Deja http://www.deja.com) for 
Don Nelson and for $10.00 he will mail you a copy of his book.
```

<a id="t-812a6a75e2"></a>
## Thread: MS-COBOL and RM-COBOL to Windows — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### MS-COBOL and RM-COBOL to Windows

- **From:** mdysli@my-dejanews.com
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hrn0l$5o2$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Hello
In the 80's a I have written hundreds of programs using Microsoft COBOL
(without SCREEN SECTION) and RM-COBOL. Today, I search a method and a
compiler ("low-cost") for a migration from MS-DOS to WINDOWS platform.
Who has a good experience in that domain ?
Thanks


--== Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/ ==--
---Share what you know. Learn what you don't.---
```

#### ↳ Re: MS-COBOL and RM-COBOL to Windows

- **From:** "Cheesle" <cheesle@online.NoSpamPlease.no>
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hs58e$fn0$1@news.cerf.net>`
- **References:** `<7hrn0l$5o2$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
mdysli@my-dejanews.com wrote in message <7hrn0l$5o2$1@nnrp1.deja.com>...
>Hello
>In the 80's a I have written hundreds of programs using Microsoft COBOL
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>Thanks

At my former employer we went from Microsoft Cobol 2.11 to Acucobol, which
made the application being running as a "native" windows app in terms of
execution. However, adopting a gui has been developed in parallel. This is
actually two questions. One is to make the application run under Windows,
which usually is straight forward with source like to tell about. Adopting a
gui is a longer run. But it may be adopted gradually as both interfaces may
live side by side.

Cheesle
```

#### ↳ Re: MS-COBOL and RM-COBOL to Windows

- **From:** "David Mowat" <david@dandm.co.nz>
- **Date:** 1999-05-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<zBw23.9254$vM1.15311@ozemail.com.au>`
- **References:** `<7hrn0l$5o2$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Hi,
Yes I have a good experience in this domain.
I have a 70,000 line COBOL programme written in Ms COBOL vs5.0 using the
screen section and broken down into 30 modules (called exes).
I did a lot of research on ways to migrate to windows, including receiving
emails from people reading this newsgroup and looking at the web sites of
all the main players. I settled for Flexus SP2. I use it for the screen
design & screen handling in place of the screen section and ACCEPT/DISPLAY
statements. I had to add some code in my modules to interface between my
existing procedures and sections and the files containing the windows
screens. The interface code is quite simple not requiring any event-driven
or OO code, It is just a loop testing for termination of field data-entry. I
can upgrade my compiler to 32 bit if I need to or keep using the command
line Ms COBOL compiler. The NT4 windows-terminal users dont know that it is
a just a 16bit app. I can change to a Fujitsu 32bit compiler from Ms (MF) if
I want to! (could be cheaper!). I can develop new modules using my new
scheme (SP2 + the continued style of code) and ad them to the app. I can
migrate to windows module by module as I have time.
For me this is total flexibility, minimal learning and minimal change. The
market believes in Windows so all they need is a windows front end, the
tested COBOL code behind the scenes can stay exactly the same. If we want to
we can put CORBA and JAVA on the side to migrate it to the web, so we are
not limited there. I dont know of any disadvantages to this approach, I
havent found any.
David Mowat
david@dandm.co.nz


mdysli@my-dejanews.com wrote in message <7hrn0l$5o2$1@nnrp1.deja.com>...
>Hello
>In the 80's a I have written hundreds of programs using Microsoft COBOL
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>---Share what you know. Learn what you don't.---
```

<a id="t-435a645a24"></a>
## Thread: Help needed for learning COBOL. — 14 msgs

_Topics: [`Tutorials, books, learning`](topics.md#learning) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help needed for learning COBOL.

- **From:** Paul Richards <Paul@dunvegan1.demon.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<j96y+NAPqUQ3Ewi6@dunvegan1.demon.co.uk>`

```
Hi,
I have a friend who asked me if I could go on the Internet and ask
around to see why his program doesn't work and how to fix it.  I myself
no nothing about COBOL but I can program in C++.  Anyway, here's his
source code:

       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. VIDVAL.
       AUTHOR. N TRIPNEY.
      *
       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
       FILE-CONTROL.
           SELECT INPUTFILE
               ASSIGN TO DISK
               ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.
           SELECT VALIDFILE
               ASSIGN TO DISK
               ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.
           SELECT ERROREPORT
               ASSIGN TO DISK
               ORGANIZATION IS SEQUENTIAL.
      *
       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.
       FD INPUTFILE
           VALUE OF FILE-ID "C:\COBOL\PROGRAM\INPUTF".
       01 INPUT-REC.
           02 VID-NO-POS.
               03 VID-NO-POS1  PIC 9.
               03 VID-NO-POS2  PIC 9.
               03 VID-NO-POS3  PIC 9.
               03 VID-NO-POS4  PIC 9.
               03 VID-NO-POS5  PIC 9.
               03 VID-NO-POS6  PIC 9.

           02 VIDFILM-NUM REDEFINES VID-NO-POS     PIC 9(6).
               88 VIDFILM-NUM-IN-RANGE     VALUE 1 THRU 999998.

           02 CHECK-DIG        PIC X.

           02 VIDFILM-TITLE    PIC X(30).

           02 VIDFILM-CAT      PIC X(3).
               88 VIDFILM-CAT-VALID
               VALUE "COM" "HOR" "DRA" "FAM" "ADU".

           02 VIDEOISS-DAT     PIC 9(8).

           02 VIDEO-COST       PIC 99V99.
               88 VIDEO-COST-IN-RANGE  VALUE 0 THRU 5000.

       FD VALIDFILE
           VALUE OF FILE-ID "C:\COBOL\PROGRAM\VALIDF".

       01 VALID-REC.
           02 VR-VIDFILM-NO    PIC 9(6).
           02 VR-VIDFILM-TITLE PIC X(30).
           02 VR-VIDFILM-CAT   PIC X(3).
           02 VR-VIDEOISS-DAT  PIC 9(8).
           02 VR-VIDEO-COST    PIC 99V99.

       01 VALID-TOTALS.
           02 VR-TOTALVIDEO-DUM-NUM    PIC 9(6).
           02 VR-TOTALVIDEO-VAL-RECS   PIC ZZZ9.
           02 VR-TOTALVIDEO-PUR-PRICE  PIC ZZZ9V99.
           02 VR-TOTALVIDEO-FILLER     PIC X(30)   VALUE SPACES.

       FD ERROREPORT
           VALUE OF FILE-ID "C:\COBOL\PROGRAM\ERREP".

       01 PRINT-REC    PIC X(80).

      *
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01 PRINT-LINES.
           02 PAGE-HEADER.
               03          PIC X(30)    VALUE SPACES.
               03          PIC X(19)    VALUE "VIDEO FILE VALIDATE".
               03          PIC X(23)    VALUE SPACES.
               03  P-S-DD  PIC 9(2).
               03          PIC X        VALUE "/".
               03  P-S-MM  PIC 9(2).
               03          PIC X        VALUE "/".
               03  P-S-YY  PIC 9(2).

           02 HEADING-LINE2.
               03          PIC X(30)    VALUE SPACES.
               03          PIC X(19)    VALUE "*******************".
               03          PIC X(31)    VALUE SPACES.

           02 SUB-HEADER.
               03          PIC X(8)     VALUE "VIDEO No".
               03          PIC X(3)     VALUE SPACES.
               03          PIC X(2)     VALUE "CD".
               03          PIC X(7)     VALUE SPACES.
               03          PIC X(5)     VALUE "TITLE".
               03          PIC X(28)    VALUE SPACES.
               03          PIC X(8)     VALUE "CATEGORY".
               03          PIC X(4)     VALUE SPACES.
               03          PIC X(6)     VALUE "ISSUED".
               03          PIC X(4)     VALUE SPACES.
               03          PIC X(5)     VALUE "PRICE".

           02 REC-LINE.
               03  PR-VID-NO       PIC 9(6).
               03                  PIC X(6)     VALUE SPACES.
               03  PR-CHK-DIG      PIC X.
               03                  PIC X(7)     VALUE SPACES.
               03  PR-VID-TITLE    PIC X(30).
               03                  PIC X(5)     VALUE SPACES.
               03  PR-VID-CAT      PIC X(3).
               03                  PIC X(6)     VALUE SPACES.
               03  PR-ISS-DATE     PIC 9(8).
               03                  PIC X(4)     VALUE SPACES.
               03  PR-PUR-PRICE    PIC 99V99.

           02 ASTERISK-LINE.
               03  ASTERISK-VID-NO     PIC X(6).
               03                      PIC X(6) VALUE SPACES.
               03  ASTERISK-CHK-DIG    PIC X.
               03                      PIC X(7) VALUE SPACES.
               03  ASTERISK-VID-TITLE  PIC X(30).
               03                      PIC X(5) VALUE SPACES.
               03  ASTERISK-VID-CAT    PIC X(3).
               03                      PIC X(6) VALUE SPACES.
               03  ASTERISK-ISS-DATE   PIC X(8).
               03                      PIC X(4) VALUE SPACES.
               03  ASTERISK-VIDEO-COST PIC X(4).

           02 TOTALS-LINE1.
               03                          PIC X(20)    VALUE SPACES.
               03
               PIC X(23)    VALUE "NUMBER OF INPUT RECORDS".
               03                          PIC X(4)     VALUE SPACES.
               03                          PIC X        VALUE "=".
               03                          PIC X(2)     VALUE SPACES.
               03  FIN-COUNT-INPUT-RECS    PIC Z(3)9.
               03                          PIC X(26)    VALUE SPACES.

           02 TOTALS-LINE2.
               03                          PIC X(20)    VALUE SPACES.
               03
               PIC X(25)    VALUE "NUMBER OF INVALID RECORDS".
               03                          PIC X(2)     VALUE SPACES.
               03                          PIC X        VALUE "=".
               03                          PIC X(2)     VALUE SPACES.
               03  FIN-COUNT-INVALID-RECS  PIC Z(3)9.
               03                          PIC X(24)    VALUE SPACES.

           02 TOTALS-LINE3.
               03                          PIC X(20)    VALUE SPACES.
               03
               PIC X(23)    VALUE "NUMBER OF VALID RECORDS".
               03                          PIC X(4)     VALUE SPACES.
               03                          PIC X        VALUE "=".
               03                          PIC X(2)     VALUE SPACES.
               03  FIN-COUNT-VALID-RECS    PIC Z(3)9.
               03                          PIC X(26)    VALUE SPACES.

       01 CALCULATION-VARIABLES.
           02  COUNT-INPUT-RECS            PIC 9(4)    VALUE 0.
           02  COUNT-INVALID-RECS          PIC 9(4)    VALUE 0.
           02  COUNT-VALID-RECS            PIC 9(4)    VALUE 0.
           02  COUNT-TOTAL-COST            PIC 9(4)V99 VALUE 0.
           02  CALC1                       PIC 999.
           02  CALC2                       PIC 9.
           02  PROG-CHK-DIG                PIC X.
           02  QUOTIENT                    PIC 999.
           02  REM                         PIC 9.


       01 ODDMENTS.
           02 LAST-VIDFILM-NUM             PIC 9(6)    VALUE 0.
           02 EOF-INDIC                    PIC 9       VALUE 0.
           02 LINE-COUNT                   PIC 99      VALUE 8.
               88 PAGE-FULL                            VALUE 8.
           02 SYSTEM-DATE.
               03 S-YY                     PIC 99.
               03 S-MM                     PIC 99.
               03 S-DD                     PIC 99.
           02 ERROR-INDICATOR              PIC 9.

      *

       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
       MAIN.
           PERFORM 1-PROG-INIT
           PERFORM 2-PROG-VALIDATE-RECS UNTIL EOF-INDIC = '1'
           PERFORM 3-PROG-TERM
       STOP RUN
       .


       1-PROG-INIT.
           OPEN INPUT INPUTFILE
               OUTPUT VALIDFILE
               OUTPUT ERROREPORT
           READ INPUTFILE
               AT END MOVE '1' TO EOF-INDIC
           END-READ
           ACCEPT SYSTEM-DATE FROM DATE
           MOVE S-YY TO P-S-YY
           MOVE S-MM TO P-S-MM
           MOVE S-DD TO P-S-DD
       .

       2-PROG-VALIDATE-RECS.
           MOVE SPACES TO ASTERISK-LINE
           MOVE '0' TO ERROR-INDICATOR
           ADD 1 TO COUNT-INPUT-RECS
           PERFORM 20-VIDFILM-NUM-CHECK
           PERFORM 21-CHECK-DIGIT-CORRECT
           PERFORM 22-CHECK-CORRECT-SEQUENCE
           PERFORM 23-VIDFILM-CAT-CHECK
           PERFORM 24-VIDEOISS-DAT-CHECK
           PERFORM 25-VIDEO-COST-CHECK
           IF ERROR-INDICATOR='0'
               PERFORM 26-WRITE-VALID-REC
           ELSE
               PERFORM 27-WRITE-INVALID-REC
           END-IF
           READ INPUTFILE
               AT END MOVE '1' TO EOF-INDIC
           END-READ
       .

       20-VIDFILM-NUM-CHECK.
           IF VIDFILM-NUM NOT NUMERIC OR NOT VIDFILM-NUM-IN-RANGE
               MOVE ALL '*' TO ASTERISK-VID-NO
               MOVE '1' TO ERROR-INDICATOR
           END-IF
       .

       21-CHECK-DIGIT-CORRECT.
           COMPUTE CALC1   =(VID-NO-POS1*6)
                           +(VID-NO-POS2*5)
                           +(VID-NO-POS3*4)
                           +(VID-NO-POS4*3)
                           +(VID-NO-POS5*2)
                           +(VID-NO-POS6*1)
           DIVIDE 7 INTO CALC1
               GIVING QUOTIENT REMAINDER REM
           SUBTRACT REM FROM 7 GIVING CALC2
           EVALUATE CALC2
               WHEN 1
                       MOVE 'A' TO PROG-CHK-DIG
               WHEN 2
                       MOVE 'B' TO PROG-CHK-DIG
               WHEN 3
                       MOVE 'C' TO PROG-CHK-DIG
               WHEN 4
                       MOVE 'D' TO PROG-CHK-DIG
               WHEN 5
                       MOVE 'E' TO PROG-CHK-DIG
               WHEN 6
                       MOVE 'F' TO PROG-CHK-DIG
               WHEN 7
                       MOVE 'G' TO PROG-CHK-DIG
           END-EVALUATE

           IF PROG-CHK-DIG NOT = CHECK-DIG
               MOVE '*' TO ASTERISK-CHK-DIG
               MOVE '1' TO ERROR-INDICATOR
           END-IF
       .

       22-CHECK-CORRECT-SEQUENCE.
           IF VIDFILM-NUM NOT > LAST-VIDFILM-NUM
               MOVE ALL '*' TO ASTERISK-VID-NO
               MOVE '1' TO ERROR-INDICATOR
           ELSE
               MOVE VIDFILM-NUM TO LAST-VIDFILM-NUM
           END-IF
       .

       23-VIDFILM-CAT-CHECK.
           IF NOT VIDFILM-CAT-VALID
               MOVE ALL '*' TO ASTERISK-VID-CAT
               MOVE '1' TO ERROR-INDICATOR
           END-IF
       .

       24-VIDEOISS-DAT-CHECK.
           IF VIDEOISS-DAT NOT NUMERIC
               MOVE ALL '*' TO ASTERISK-ISS-DATE
               MOVE '1' TO ERROR-INDICATOR
           END-IF
       .

       25-VIDEO-COST-CHECK.
           IF VIDEO-COST NOT NUMERIC OR NOT VIDEO-COST-IN-RANGE
               MOVE ALL '*' TO ASTERISK-VIDEO-COST
               MOVE '1' TO ERROR-INDICATOR
           END-IF
       .

       26-WRITE-VALID-REC.
           MOVE VIDFILM-NUM TO VR-VIDFILM-NO
           MOVE VIDFILM-TITLE TO VR-VIDFILM-TITLE
           MOVE VIDFILM-CAT TO VR-VIDFILM-CAT
           MOVE VIDEOISS-DAT TO VR-VIDEOISS-DAT
           MOVE VIDEO-COST TO VR-VIDEO-COST
           WRITE VALID-REC
           ADD 1 TO COUNT-VALID-RECS
           ADD VIDEO-COST TO COUNT-TOTAL-COST
       .

       27-WRITE-INVALID-REC.
           IF PAGE-FULL
               WRITE PRINT-REC FROM PAGE-HEADER AFTER PAGE
               WRITE PRINT-REC FROM HEADING-LINE2 AFTER 1
               WRITE PRINT-REC FROM SUB-HEADER AFTER 2
               MOVE 0 TO LINE-COUNT
           END-IF
           MOVE VIDFILM-NUM TO PR-VID-NO
           MOVE CHECK-DIG TO PR-CHK-DIG
           MOVE VIDFILM-TITLE TO PR-VID-TITLE
           MOVE VIDFILM-CAT TO PR-VID-CAT
           MOVE VIDEOISS-DAT TO PR-ISS-DATE
           MOVE VIDEO-COST TO PR-PUR-PRICE
           WRITE PRINT-REC FROM REC-LINE AFTER 2
           WRITE PRINT-REC FROM ASTERISK-LINE AFTER 1
           ADD 1 TO LINE-COUNT
           ADD 1 TO COUNT-INVALID-RECS
       .

       3-PROG-TERM
           MOVE 999999 TO VR-TOTALVIDEO-DUM-NUM
           MOVE COUNT-VALID-RECS TO VR-TOTALVIDEO-VAL-RECS
           MOVE COUNT-TOTAL-COST TO VR-TOTALVIDEO-PUR-PRICE
           MOVE SPACES TO VR-TOTALVIDEO-FILLER
           WRITE VALID-TOTALS
           MOVE COUNT-INPUT-RECS TO FIN-COUNT-INPUT-RECS
           MOVE COUNT-INVALID-RECS TO FIN-COUNT-INVALID-RECS
           MOVE COUNT-VALID-RECS TO FIN-COUNT-VALID-RECS
           WRITE PRINT-REC FROM TOTALS-LINE1 AFTER 5
           WRITE PRINT-REC FROM TOTALS-LINE2 AFTER 1
           WRITE PRINT-REC FROM TOTALS-LINE3 AFTER 1
           CLOSE INPUTFILE VALIDFILE ERROREPORT
       .

COBOL isn't really a programming language, is it?  :)
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help needed for learning COBOL.

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@aiservices.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hubu2$lfe$1@server.cntfl.com>`
- **References:** `<j96y+NAPqUQ3Ewi6@dunvegan1.demon.co.uk> <3741eec2.14806022@news2.humboldt1.com> <5OaEyFAPynQ3EwTa@dunvegan1.demon.co.uk>`

```

Paul Richards wrote in message <5OaEyFAPynQ3EwTa@dunvegan1.demon.co.uk>...
>Kindrick Ownby writes,
>>On Tue, 18 May 1999 12:10:07 +0100, Paul Richards
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>>>no nothing about COBOL but I can program in C++.  [...]
[...]
>>In merely scrolling thru the program I noticed that the paragraph
>>label  3-PROG-TERM is not terminated with a period.
>K, I'll tell him.  Is that the name of a function of something "3-PROG-
>TERM"?  If it is it's a bit meaningful ain't it?

A paragraph in COBOL has an equivalence to a static function
in C++ with the restriction that the function has no return value, no
parameters, and no local variables. For any paragraph (in a
structured COBOL program), the equivalent C++ function
declaration would look like:

static void paragraph_name (void) {
    // code for paragraph body
}

Then
    PERFORM PARAGRAPH-NAME
is equivalent to
    paragraph_name ();

With the name "3-PROG-TERM", I would expect the paragraph
to contain program termination code. It does; therefore, the name
is sufficiently meaningful, for me.

HTH
------------------
Rick Smith
```

<a id="t-885c7b8c9f"></a>
## Thread: Imbedded keys with MF 3.25 on Unix. — 3 msgs

### Imbedded keys with MF 3.25 on Unix.

- **From:** pduboisREMOVETHIS@enteract.com (PAL3000)
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3741d467.33916450@news.enteract.com>`

```
Hello, 

We are getting some weird lockups during the loading of a flat file
into an indexed file.  The indexed file has keys setup like the
following:

    01 ALT-KEY-3.
        03 FLD-A                                PIC X(10).
        03 FLD-B                                PIC X(10).
        03 ALT-KEY-2.
            05 FLD-C                            PIC X(10).
            05 FLD-D                            PIC X(10).
            05 ALT-KEY-1.
                07 FLD-E                        PIC X(10).
                07 MAIN-KEY                PIC X(10).

The field names and sizes are just examples.

The select statement had the main key of the file as MAIN-KEY.  This
is the unique key.  There are 3 alternate keys without duplicates as
ALT-KEY-1, ALT-KEY-2, and ALT-KEY-3.

What is you opinion of having keys like this? Is it safe?  

I am suspecting that since our file is very large (appx 1,500,000
records), these embedded keys are causing the load to lock up, and
even corrupt the indexes.

Thanks, and feel free to ask questions.

Paul
--------------------------------------
To reply by email, please take out the
words REMOVETHIS from my email address
```

#### ↳ Re: Imbedded keys with MF 3.25 on Unix.

- **From:** "Rick Price" <rick@hpdi.demon.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-05-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<927104530.27395.0.nnrp-04.9e98b131@news.demon.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<3741d467.33916450@news.enteract.com>`

```
We have files with similar structures without any problems.

What do you mean by locked up?  Do you get a file status?

How big are your records?  You can't have a file larger than 2gb.  If your
MF is defaulting to lock mode 2 then you can't have shared files larger than
1gb.  These are unix limits.  (I'm assuming you're on unix).  If you have
lock_mode=0 then shared files can also be 2gb.

Rick

PAL3000 wrote in message <3741d467.33916450@news.enteract.com>...
>Hello,
>
…[31 more quoted lines elided]…
>words REMOVETHIS from my email address
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Imbedded keys with MF 3.25 on Unix.

- **From:** pduboisREMOVETHIS@enteract.com (PAL3000)
- **Date:** 1999-05-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<37447e16.26827164@news.enteract.com>`
- **References:** `<3741d467.33916450@news.enteract.com> <927104530.27395.0.nnrp-04.9e98b131@news.demon.co.uk>`

```
Thanks for the reply.

We found the problem was due to some corrupted input records.

Paul


On Wed, 19 May 1999 10:03:38 +0100, "Rick Price"
<rick@hpdi.demon.co.uk> wrote:

>We have files with similar structures without any problems.
>
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…

words REMOVETHIS from my email address

<a id="t-0a2ed254dd"></a>
## Thread: Bug alert Fujitsu ! — 5 this month / 7 total

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Bug alert Fujitsu !

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hs2pe$kjf$1@news.igs.net>`

```
I suspect that a bug I just discovered in Fujitsu (3 and 4) is affecting
a whole lot of people.  It is in the cbl_copy_file routine, that often gets
used
as a spool method.

The routine crashes both Windows95 and 98 if the file being copied goes
beyond
2*64 bytes.  Here is the code I have been using.  It will work fine as long
as the file named in FILENAME-IN is less than 2*64k bytes long. As soon
as the file being copied exceeds that length, it causes a machine reset.

If you are using the copy subroutine, you have a pending bug.

000010 IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
000020 PROGRAM-ID. "_NEWSPLR".
000030 AUTHOR. D. L. Tees.
000040 SECURITY.
000050     Copyright(C), 1999, DONALD L TEES.
000060 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
000070 CONFIGURATION SECTION.
000080 SOURCE-COMPUTER. IBM-PC.
000090 OBJECT-COMPUTER. IBM-PC.
000100 SPECIAL-NAMES.
000110 INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
000120 FILE-CONTROL.
000130 DATA DIVISION.
000140 FILE SECTION.
000150 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
000160 LINKAGE SECTION.
000170 01 FILENAME-IN       PICTURE X(40).
000180 01 PRINTER-NAME-IN   PICTURE X(40).
000190 PROCEDURE DIVISION USING FILENAME-IN PRINTER-NAME-IN.
000200 MAIN-LINE.
000210     CALL "CBL_COPY_FILE" USING
000220                          BY REFERENCE FILENAME-IN
000230                                       PRINTER-NAME-IN
000240                          END-CALL.
000250     EXIT PROGRAM.

```

#### ↳ Re: Bug alert Fujitsu !

- **From:** "Russell Styles" <RWSTYLES@COMPUSERVE.COM>
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<OVVcZRYo#GA.275@nih2naab.prod2.compuserve.com>`
- **References:** `<7hs2pe$kjf$1@news.igs.net>`

```
Just what do we mean by 2*64?  Is this a bug that we should worry about, or
a bug that will never happen in this lifetime.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Bug alert Fujitsu !

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1999-05-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hub9t$1h9$1@news.igs.net>`
- **References:** `<7hs2pe$kjf$1@news.igs.net> <OVVcZRYo#GA.275@nih2naab.prod2.compuserve.com>`

```
I meant 2*16.  I think I am getting senile.  65,000 roughly.  It is just big
enough
that it will probably get through testing, and crash in the first real world
test.

Sorry about that.

>Just what do we mean by 2*64?  Is this a bug that we should worry about, or
>a bug that will never happen in this lifetime.
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

## Thread: ODBC -> Cobol — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### ODBC -> Cobol

- **From:** Anton Rusbach <a.rusbach@coss.nl>
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<37417C96.F48BB2D6@coss.nl>`

```
I heard of an ODBC driver which allows products like MSAccess to
communicate with conventional Cobol files.

What Cobol variants are supported? 
Who supplies this product?
How is the performance?
What are the experiences working with the product?
```

#### ↳ Re: ODBC -> Cobol

- **From:** "Cheesle" <cheesle@online.NoSpamPlease.no>
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hs5ek$fno$1@news.cerf.net>`
- **References:** `<37417C96.F48BB2D6@coss.nl>`

```
Anton Rusbach wrote in message <37417C96.F48BB2D6@coss.nl>...
>I heard of an ODBC driver which allows products like MSAccess to
>communicate with conventional Cobol files.

Acucobol has this opportunity.

>Who supplies this product?

Take a look at www.acucorp.com

>How is the performance?

Reasonable, not as fast as native access of course, but good enough. This is
actually a very difficult question as it will depend a lot on what computer
equipment you have.

>What are the experiences working with the product?

I haven't used it for anything but experimenting, I am sure if you contact
them, you will be advised to references.

Cheelse
```

Check out The Flexus COBOL Page at http://www.flexus.com
```

<a id="t-2575f6f366"></a>
## Thread: printing from NT Workstation from RMCOBOL — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### printing from NT Workstation from RMCOBOL

- **From:** john@clswin.com
- **Date:** 1999-05-18T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hsoes$s9t$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
Wondering if anyone else has experienced the problem we are facing. 

We have
just recently switched from the 16-bit version Runtime version 6.08 to the
32-bit Runtime v 6.51 for our application. When a user tries to print from
our application from a NT Workstation using Novell's Client for NT v 4.x, the
program hangs. In discussing with RM, they aknowleded that they are getting a
different result when their runtime is going a Windows GETFILETYPE 32bit API
call than what they were expecting. They are expecting a File Type of
"Character Device", and instead with this workstation client they are getting
a file type of "Disk Device".

RM is telling me that we are the only one's
reporting this problem to them and they feel it is a Novell problem because
the return code from the API call is not correct. 

I would appreciate any
feedback you may have. If you are also experiencing these problems, please
contact Liant.

Thanks. 

Has anyone else been successful in printing from a
NT Workstation using Novell Client 4.X?  If so, I would greatly appreciate
hearing from you. I feel this is a RM issue and as such they should step up
to the plate and fix it.


--== Sent via Deja.com http://www.deja.com/ ==--
---Share what you know. Learn what you don't.---
```

#### ↳ Re: printing from NT Workstation from RMCOBOL

- **From:** rtwolfe@flexus.com (Bob Wolfe)
- **Date:** 1999-05-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3742b784.4832995@news.enter.net>`
- **References:** `<7hsoes$s9t$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
john@clswin.com wrote:

>Wondering if anyone else has experienced the problem we are facing. 

We have
>just recently switched from the 16-bit version Runtime version 6.08 to the
>32-bit Runtime v 6.51 for our application. When a user tries to print from
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>a file type of "Disk Device".

RM is telling me that we are the only one's
>reporting this problem to them and they feel it is a Novell problem because
>the return code from the API call is not correct. 

I would appreciate any
>feedback you may have. If you are also experiencing these problems, please
>contact Liant.

Thanks. 

Has anyone else been successful in printing from a
>NT Workstation using Novell Client 4.X?  If so, I would greatly appreciate
>hearing from you. I feel this is a RM issue and as such they should step up
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>---Share what you know. Learn what you don't.---

FormPrint supports network printing from RM COBOL.  It does require
that your client machine is running in Windows and uses the Windows
Print Manager on the client machine.

If that is a bad assumption, then I apologize.

Access http://www.flexus.com and follow the links for flexus products
and then foprmprint.





Bob Wolfe, flexus
Check out The Flexus COBOL Page at http://www.flexus.com
```

#### ↳ Re: printing from NT Workstation from RMCOBOL

- **From:** "Cheesle" <cheesle@online.NoSpamPlease.no>
- **Date:** 1999-05-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7hugnh$coe$1@news.cerf.net>`
- **References:** `<7hsoes$s9t$1@nnrp1.deja.com>`

```
john@clswin.com wrote in message <7hsoes$s9t$1@nnrp1.deja.com>...
>Wondering if anyone else has experienced the problem we are facing. We have
>just recently switched from the 16-bit version Runtime version 6.08 to the
>32-bit Runtime v 6.51 for our application. When a user tries to print from
>our application from a NT Workstation using Novell's Client for NT v 4.x,
the

I have no experience with RM Cobol, but I have a lot of experience with
Windows 95/NT and Novell, and it is a pain in the, yes you know where. Make
sure you have the absolute latest updated drivers. There has been numerous
bugfixes both from Microsoft and Novell, so, it may very well be that RM has
right. Other than that. If this has been narrowed down to the level that
they know what function is failing and that it is providing an unexpected
response. It should be fairly easy for RM to contact Novell and ask them. I
consider they should have enough evidence to start working. They might need
the object description entry for the printer as well.

Cheesle
```
